description:
If running the Trivial File Transfer Protocol (TFTP) service is necessary, it should be configured to change its root directory at startup. To do so, find the path for the tftp systemd service: 
$ sudo systemctl show tftp | grep ExecStart=
ExecStart={ path=/usr/sbin/in.tftpd ; argv[]=/usr/sbin/in.tftpd -s /var/lib/tftpboot ; ignore_errors=no ; start_time=[n/a] ; stop_time=[n/a] ; pid=0 ; code=(null) ; status=0/0 }e
and ensure the ExecStart line on that file includes the -s option with a subdirectory: 
ExecStart=/usr/sbin/in.tftpd -s
rationale:
Using the <tt>-s</tt> option causes the TFTP service to only serve files from the
given directory. Serving files from an intentionally-specified directory
reduces the risk of sharing files which should remain private.
ocil_clause: the ExecStart property of tftp does not contain correctly set -s flag
ocil:
Use sudo systemctl show tftp to verify that tftp service is using secure mode. 
$ sudo systemctl show tftp | grep ExecStart=
ExecStart={ path=/usr/sbin/in.tftpd ; argv[]=/usr/sbin/in.tftpd -s /var/lib/tftpboot ; ignore_errors=no ; start_time=[n/a] ; stop_time=[n/a] ; pid=0 ; code=(null) ; status=0/0 }e
and ensure the ExecStart line on that file includes the -s option with a subdirectory: 
ExecStart=/usr/sbin/in.tftpd -s

stig.srg_requirement: If the Trivial File Transfer Protocol (TFTP) server is required, the Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 TFTP daemon must be configured to operate in secure mode.
stig.checktext:
If a TFTP server is not installed, this rule is not applicable.

Check to see if TFTP server is installed with the following command:

$ sudo dnf list installed | grep tftp-server
tftp-server.x86_64 5.2-49.el10

Verify that the TFTP daemon, if tftp.server is installed, is configured to operate in secure mode with the following command:

$ systemctl cat tftp.service | grep -i execstart
ExecStart=/usr/sbin/in.tftpd -s /var/lib/tftpboot

Note: The "-s" option ensures that the TFTP server only serves files from the specified directory, which is a security measure to prevent unauthorized access to other parts of the file system.

If the TFTP server is installed but the TFTP daemon is not configured to operate in secure mode, this is a finding.
stig.fixtext:
Configure the TFTP daemon to operate in secure mode with the following command:
$ sudo systemctl edit tftp.service

In the editor enter:
[Service]
ExecStart=/usr/sbin/in.tftpd -s /var/lib/tftpboot

After making changes, reload the systemd daemon and restart the TFTP service as follows:
$ sudo systemctl daemon-reload
$ sudo systemctl restart tftp.service
stig.vuldiscussion: Restricting TFTP to a specific directory prevents remote users from copying, transferring, or overwriting system files.

title: Ensure tftp systemd Service Uses Secure Mode
